events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence violation: instruments not isolated before hydro. Pressure testing per ASME B31.3 §345. Need decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Instruments not isolated before hydro can damage instruments, gauges, transmitters; potential failure, inaccurate readings, safety hazard. Likely high or critical. Since hydro test involves high pressure water, unisolated instruments could rupture or cause injury. I'd set risk high. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective? The observed condition category applies: installed-defective because instruments not isolated is a defect in installed state. Could also non-compliant. Use installed-defective and non-compliant maybe. The schema says component_conditions categories may apply: installed-defective. We can include installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60. Value not asked -> 0. Sound_source etc default empty string? Schema says string. Could use "" and 0.0. Order not audio task but sequence check; maybe include order of steps? The task says check order of work and catch sequence violation. Required output includes order: ["step-id", "..."] and workable: ["activity ID", "..."]. Need populate with plausible step IDs. Could define order: isolate instruments, install test gauge, fill/vent, pressurize, hold, inspect, depressurize, drain. Since violation is instruments not isolated before hydro, the correct order should have isolation before hydro. We can list order IDs: ["P-ISO-INSTR", "P-INSTALL-TEST-GAUGE", "P-FILL-VENT", "P-PRESSURIZE", "P-HOLD", "P-INSPECT", "P-DEPRESSURIZE", "P-DRAIN"]. Workable activities maybe after isolation: ["P-INSTALL-TEST-GAUGE", "P-FILL-VENT", ...]. Findings: instruments not isolated before hydro test; pressure gauges/control devices exposed to test pressure; risk of instrument damage and erroneous readings; potential personnel injury if instrument fails. Actions: isolate, remove, or bypass instruments not rated for test pressure; install calibrated test gauges at proper locations; verify isolation with line list/P&ID; re-energize only after test completion; hold hydro until isolation confirmed. Rationale: ASME B31.3 §345 requires components not subjected to test pressure be isolated/removed; instruments not rated/test-capable must be isolated before pressurization to prevent damage and ensure accurate test. References: ASME B31.3 §345.2.2? Actually §345.2 Test Requirements: all joints exposed, equipment isolated, expansion joints etc. Need not quote exact. Provide references list. Ensure JSON valid. Need no prose outside JSON.

Leaving instruments connected during hydro exposes them to test pressure, creating a safety hazard and invalidating the test. The correct sequence is isolation first, then pressurization.","references":["ASME B31.3 §345"]}}
